llm_input:
You are the local LLM extractor for the AP Automation system.
Return only one JSON object. Do not include Markdown, prose, or code fences.
Extract facts for the extraction.v1 schema below. The deterministic rules engine will make final routing decisions.
Do not invent property codes, destinations, email recipients, or invoice facts. Use null for absent facts.
Set confidence below 0.90 when required invoice, property, or business-unit facts are incomplete or uncertain.
Use document.document_flags for high-risk conditions such as link_only_invoice, missing_invoice_attachment, past_due, ach_or_auto_draft, ben_e_keith, conflicting_signals, or low_text_quality.
Allowed document_type values: invoice, statement, account_summary, contract, pay_application, vendor_question, payment_inquiry, past_due_notice, ach_notice, auto_draft_notice, ben_e_keith_notice, lien_release, unknown.
Set extractor.type to "codex_cli", extractor.name to "codex_exec", extractor.model to null unless known, and extractor.prompt_version to "local_msg_extraction.v1".

Return this exact JSON shape and field names:
{
  "schema_version": "extraction.v1",
  "extractor": {"type": "codex_cli", "name": "codex_exec", "model": null, "prompt_version": "local_msg_extraction.v1"},
  "email": {"subject": "string or null", "sender_email": "string or null", "received_at": "ISO-8601 string or null"},
  "document": {
    "document_type": "invoice|statement|account_summary|contract|pay_application|vendor_question|payment_inquiry|past_due_notice|ach_notice|auto_draft_notice|ben_e_keith_notice|lien_release|unknown",
    "document_flags": [],
    "requires_attachment": true,
    "has_invoice_attachment": true,
    "link_only": false,
    "multi_invoice": false,
    "requires_merge": false
  },
  "invoice": {
    "invoice_number": "string or null",
    "invoice_date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
    "due_date": "YYYY-MM-DD or null",
    "amount": 0.0,
    "currency": "USD or null",
    "vendor_name": "string or null",
    "vendor_email": "string or null",
    "bill_to": "string or null",
    "property_code": "string or null",
    "property_name": "string or null",
    "service_address": "string or null"
  },
  "business_signals": {"business_unit_code": "string or null", "possible_property_aliases": [], "subject_instruction_hint": "string or null"},
  "confidence": {"overall": 0.0, "document_type": 0.0, "invoice_fields": 0.0, "property_identity": 0.0, "business_unit": 0.0},
  "evidence": {"summary": "short explanation of extracted facts", "source_attachments": [], "source_pages": []}
}
Required fields must be present even when values are null, false, 0.0, or empty arrays. Use invoice.amount, not amount_due. Use confidence.invoice_fields and confidence.property_identity. Use evidence.summary, not an evidence array or arbitrary evidence map.

Input email and attachment metadata:
{
  "attachments": [
    {
      "content_type": null,
      "file_name": "Invoic~1.pdf",
      "file_size_bytes": 101608,
      "sha256": "042d8130c3e2a3ca90c4b5bf9ce4ba58669b91eba02e5e398301d56d71203b3b",
      "storage_path": "local/attachments/8c03adea-36f1-48c7-b699-cca4c6d70b17/001-Invoic_1.pdf"
    },
    {
      "content_type": "image/png",
      "file_name": "image001.png",
      "file_size_bytes": 8324,
      "sha256": "666bbd84668ae9d10da3d1a8d3b60351acb22b6b02ba8ef26cbb8092ae37c8a2",
      "storage_path": "local/attachments/8c03adea-36f1-48c7-b699-cca4c6d70b17/002-image001.png"
    }
  ],
  "email": {
    "body_text": "Attached is an example of an invoice received for a property that is 3rd Party Managed by Hillwood and processed via a different payables software system than Medius.  The property address is reviewed and sent to the designated associate who manages payables for that specific building. 

 

I have a spreadsheet that details Hillwood owned buildings, 3rd Party managed buildings, and their associated addresses, that is referenced, as the AP Inbox is worked through.  I can send you a copy of this for refence as well.   I imagine the AI feature will need this to learn address specifics?

 

This invoice would be forwarded to Michele.Fellers@hillwood.com <mailto:Michele.Fellers@hillwood.com>  for processing.

 

Leslie Rodriguez

Accounts Payable Coordinator 

Hillwood, A Perot Company

Direct: 817.224.6343 | Leslie.Rodriguez@Hillwood.com <mailto:Leslie.Rodriguez@Hillwood.com> 

 

From: Miner, Casey <casey.miner@hillwood.com> 
Sent: Wednesday, March 4, 2026 4:45 PM
To: PropertiesAP <Propertiesap@hillwood.com>
Cc: Topasna, Denise <denise.topasna@hillwood.com>; Vilbig, Megan <megan.vilbig@hillwood.com>
Subject: Attached is your invoice #2857 from Alliance Landscape Co LLC

 

Attached is Invoice #2857 for work completed at Gateway 51.

 

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to reach out. 

 

Thank you and have a wonderful day!

 

Casey Miner

Office Manager

Alliance Landscape Company

Hillwood, A Perot Company

3730 Haslet-Roanoke Rd., Roanoke, TX 76262

Direct: 817.224.6044 | AllianceLandscapeCompany.com <http://alliancelandscapecompany.com/> 

Mobile: 817.929.1068 | casey.miner@hillwood.com <mailto:casey.miner@hillwood.com> 

 



",
    "received_at": null,

email.subject: FW: Attached is your invoice #2857 from Alliance Landscape Co LLC

evidence.summary: Email thread says Invoice #2857 from Alliance Landscape Co LLC is attached for work completed at Gateway 51. Wrapper text describes it as an example invoice for a 3rd Party Managed property and includes a forwarding instruction, but invoice date, amount, due date, bill-to, property code, and service address are not present in the email text.
